O'LEARY ARE RESTRAINED FROM ENCUMBERING OR SELLING ALL OR ANY PORTION OF ANY OF THE REAL PROPERTIES THEY CURRENTLY OWN OTHER THAN FOR THE PURPOSES OF PAYMENT OF ATTORNEY'S FEES, COURT COSTS AND LITIGATION COSTS FOR THIS LITIGATION AND TO IMPROVE, MAINTAIN OR REPAIR THE CONDITION OF ANY SUCH PROPERTIES. Off Calendar May-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PATRICIA E. HENLE, TEL. NO. 929-3197 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHARLES J. KATZ, TEL. NO. (650) 692-4100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF CENTRAL GARDENS, INC.'S Motion For A Prohibitory Injunction To Maintain The Status Quo is Granted in part. Pending further order of the court and entry of a final judgment, Ms. Dewar and Mr. O'Leary are restrained from encumbering or selling all or any portion of any of the real properties they currently own other than for the purposes of payment of attorney's fees, court costs and litigation costs for this litigation and to improve, maintain or repair the condition of any such properties. Balance of equities favors granting such an injunction, especially since Ms. Dewar appears to admit that she engaged in conduct for which she is liable to the plaintiff, the trial date was recently continued to accommodate defendants, and the only harm identified by defendants has been addressed by the terms of the injunction. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/hk

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Patricia Henle of Law Office of Patricia E. Henle telephone number (415)- 929-3197 for Plaintiff. No other appearances.

Counsel for Plaintiff stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter was not argued. The Court adopted the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CENTRAL GARDENS, INC.'S Motion To Compel Further Deposition Time is granted in part. Plaintiff shall be allowed 10 additional hours of examination (excluding time spent on breaks and meals) of Robin Dewar. The Court finds that this case is more complicated than an average case due to the lengthy time period involved and the voluminous documents on which the witness must be questioned. The court "shall" allow additional time "if needed to fairly examine the deponent." CCP 2025.290(a); see Certainteed Corp. v. Sup.Ct. (Hart) (2014) 222 CA4th 1053, 1060, 166 CR3d 539, 544-7-hour time limit is "merely presumptive" and applies only if court does not order otherwise]. Order signed in open court.
